title: A Look At BigBear.ai (BBAI) Valuation After Q1 Results And New US$53 Million Classified Contract
text: BigBear.ai reported Q1 2026 results showing $34.44 million in sales alongside a net loss of $56.76 million. The company maintained its full-year 2026 revenue guidance between $135-165 million. The company secured a significant $53 million classified contract, bolstering its $385 million backlog and supporting its emphasis on multiyear programs. According to Simply Wall St's analysis, BBAI trades at $4.17 against a fair value estimate of $5.33—suggesting the stock is approximately 21.8% undervalued. The valuation thesis hinges on steady top line expansion and a future margin profile that looks very different from current performance. However, this optimistic view faces headwinds. The stock's price-to-sales ratio of 15.7x significantly exceeds both industry averages (2x) and peer comparables (0.3x), with a fair ratio estimated at 2.1x. Despite a 20.87% one-month gain, BBAI has declined 28.60% year-to-date while showing a stronger 3-year total return of 66.14%.
